text.title: Structured Output Basics — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure from input brief to signed-off output code.

list.item: Input: Downstream consumer's required fields.
Action: Author Pydantic model with field names, types, descriptions.
Output: schema.py with TaskResponse.
Decision gate: If schema has >20 fields, split into sub-models.

section.title: Step 2 — Build the prompt from schema

list.item: Input: schema.py.
Action: Use schema.model_json_schema() to generate the prompt's field list; instruction = «fill this schema».
Output: prompt template referencing schema fields.
Decision gate: If prompt grows beyond 1k tokens, schema is too big — split.
Input: Provider client.
Action: Pass schema as response_format (OpenAI) or tool-as-schema (Anthropic).
Output: API response constrained to the schema shape.
Decision gate: If provider doesn't support native mode, fall back to JSON mode + post-validate.
Input: Response content.
Action: Parse through Pydantic; on ValidationError inject error and retry once.
Output: Validated Pydantic instance.
Decision gate: If second attempt fails, log + fallback.
Input: Validated instance.
Action: Insert into DB; log success / failure rate to telemetry.
Output: Telemetry showing parse_success_rate > 99%.
Decision gate: If success rate drops, return to step 2 — schema or prompt drift.
